title: MRD Resistance Geometry Pilot v0

coordinate_system.radius: Evidence-to-action distance from source-backed measurement or review-supported clinical context toward unresolved hypothesis.
coordinate_system.tension: Uncertainty preserved before downstream claim upgrade.

mechanism_groups.label: MRD trajectory split
mechanism_groups.relapse_role: Research bucket for separating posttreatment clonal elimination from MRD-positive residual-state patterns.
mechanism_groups.why_it_matters: Durable-remission maps should not collapse deep response, MRD negativity, MRD positivity, and residual clone behavior into one undifferentiated state.
what_to_extract: paired diagnosis and posttreatment sample availability
limitations: Single-study mechanism extraction.
Not a patient-specific MRD interpretation.
Not a relapse-risk estimate.

mechanism_groups.label: Residual transcriptional adaptation
mechanism_groups.relapse_role: Research bucket for nongenetic cell-state adaptation in residual myeloma cells after treatment.
mechanism_groups.why_it_matters: Resistance maps that only track clone genetics may miss transcriptional states that shape residual-cell fitness.
limitations: Transcriptional adaptation is not a treatment-selection rule.
Requires source-specific methods review before cross-study comparison.

mechanism_groups.label: Metabolic resistance state
mechanism_groups.relapse_role: Research bucket for metabolism-linked survival states in residual myeloma cells.
mechanism_groups.why_it_matters: A metabolic branch gives the map a way to preserve non-target, non-immune resistance hypotheses without recommending interventions.
limitations: Metabolic signal is hypothesis-generating.
Not evidence for a metabolic treatment recommendation.

mechanism_groups.label: NF-kB selective state
mechanism_groups.relapse_role: Research bucket for pathway-state preference in selected residual plasma cells.
mechanism_groups.why_it_matters: Pathway-state extraction helps distinguish residual-cell biology from broad relapse labels.
limitations: Pathway preference is not a target-selection rule.
Needs expert review before claim upgrade.

mechanism_groups.label: Genetic and nongenetic coupling
mechanism_groups.relapse_role: Research bucket for correlation between clone architecture and transcriptional dynamics.
mechanism_groups.why_it_matters: The map should preserve both genetic and nongenetic axes when representing therapy-induced evolution.
limitations: Correlation does not establish a clinical intervention point.
Not a causal mechanism ranking.

mechanism_groups.label: Tumor microenvironment shelter interactions
mechanism_groups.relapse_role: Research bucket for interactions between residual malignant plasma cells and the marrow immune microenvironment.
mechanism_groups.why_it_matters: Residual disease may be shaped by cell interactions as well as tumor-intrinsic properties.
limitations: Interaction inference is not proof of clinical actionability.
Requires source-specific methods and expert review.

mechanism_groups.label: Subclone diversity
mechanism_groups.relapse_role: Research bucket for heterogeneous subclonal architecture and genomic instability across myeloma progression.
mechanism_groups.why_it_matters: A map of durable remission needs a place for heterogeneity before assigning mechanism priority.
limitations: Review-level context.
Not a quantified prognosis or relapse-risk model.

mechanism_groups.label: Single-cell translation gap
mechanism_groups.relapse_role: Interpretation-risk bucket for separating research resolution from clinical-readiness claims.
mechanism_groups.why_it_matters: Advanced profiling can generate rich maps while still being limited by cost, data complexity, standardization, and ethical considerations.
limitations: Not evidence that single-cell profiling is routine clinical decision support.
Not patient-specific implementation guidance.

mechanism_groups.label: T-cell therapy pressure
mechanism_groups.relapse_role: Research bucket for immune redirection pressure, target class, and unresolved sequencing questions in relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma.
mechanism_groups.why_it_matters: The geometry needs a therapy-pressure axis without turning that axis into a treatment sequence recommendation.
limitations: Review-level landscape context.
Not a product comparison or sequencing recommendation.

mechanism_groups.label: Unmet high-risk context
mechanism_groups.relapse_role: Context bucket for extramedullary disease and plasma cell leukemia as high-unmet-need settings.
mechanism_groups.why_it_matters: Geometry maps should mark context modifiers that may affect how evidence is interpreted.
limitations: Not a patient-specific risk assessment.
Needs subtype-specific expert review before claim upgrade.

nodes.observation: Posttreatment patterns include MRD-negative clonal elimination and MRD-positive clonal stabilization or selection in one single-cell study.
nodes.tension: Single-study context; do not generalize frequency or patient-specific meaning.
nodes.public_use: Separate deep response from residual clone state in research maps.
nodes.do_not_infer: No patient-specific MRD interpretation, relapse prediction, or treatment guidance.

nodes.observation: Residual myeloma can involve transcriptional adaptation in addition to genetic evolution.
nodes.tension: Mechanistic implication requires source-specific extraction and expert review.
nodes.public_use: Preserve nongenetic state fields in extraction templates.

nodes.observation: Cycling-resistant plasma cells in the cited study showed a fatty-acid-oxidation shift.
nodes.tension: Metabolism bucket is hypothesis-generating for research extraction only.
nodes.public_use: Track metabolism as a research bucket.

nodes.observation: Selective plasma cells in the cited study favored NF-kB pathway activity.
nodes.tension: Pathway signal is not a clinical target-selection rule.
nodes.public_use: Track pathway-state hypotheses with source-specific limits.

nodes.observation: The cited single-cell study found correlation between genetic and transcriptional dynamics.
nodes.tension: Correlation does not by itself identify causal intervention points.
nodes.public_use: Avoid clone-only maps when representing resistance.
nodes.do_not_infer: No causal ranking or clinical decision support.

nodes.observation: Selective plasma cells had stronger tumor-microenvironment interactions in the cited study; reviews support microenvironment tracking as a major myeloma research dimension.
nodes.tension: Interaction fields need cell-pair and assay-context extraction before ranking.
nodes.public_use: Track interaction fields before ranking mechanisms.

nodes.observation: Reviews describe dynamic subclonal diversity and genomic instability across myeloma progression.
nodes.tension: Subclonal diversity is context, not a quantified risk score in this pilot.
nodes.public_use: Keep heterogeneity visible as a structural feature.

nodes.observation: Reviews emphasize clinical-translation barriers for single-cell technologies, including cost, data complexity, standardization, and ethical considerations.
nodes.tension: Technology promise and clinical readiness must remain separated.
nodes.public_use: Keep implementation readiness separate from biological plausibility.
nodes.do_not_infer: No claim that single-cell profiling is routine clinical decision support.

nodes.observation: Current reviews describe BCMA- and GPRC5D-directed immune therapies alongside continued relapse and sequencing questions.
nodes.tension: Therapy pressure is represented as research context, not treatment sequencing.
nodes.public_use: Represent therapy pressure without recommending sequencing.
nodes.do_not_infer: No product comparison, sequence, or trial recommendation.

nodes.observation: Reviews identify extramedullary disease and plasma cell leukemia as ongoing high-unmet-need contexts.
nodes.tension: High-unmet-need contexts require expert review and subtype-specific evidence.
nodes.public_use: Mark context modifiers requiring expert review.
nodes.do_not_infer: No patient-specific risk or treatment-fit inference.

next_work: Convert nodes into source-specific extraction records.
Add expert-review questions for each coordinate and tension field.
Compare the geometry map against a flat mechanism list.
Keep all patient-specific or case-specific reasoning out of the public stream.
